mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series with an interaction term coefficient insignificant at 95 percent confidence, or generates negative returns in greater than 50 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and operational leverage, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during high-inflation periods, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict 20-day equity direction with a Rank IC below 0.01, or positive return frequency drops below 50 percent across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 190.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 190.0, or the inflation threshold loses predictive significance with a p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant real GDP growth signals a deteriorating economic backdrop that erodes consumer confidence and corporate profit forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns in greater than 55 percent of observed cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures reflect immediate consumer spending strength, which boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The consumption signal fails to predict 5-day returns during credit tightening periods, or the directional accuracy drops below 52 percent in walk-forward testing.
